description: Create a content slide on expected changes in risk management FTEs over the next three years, with a left narrative text column and a right horizontal bar chart plus a highlighted 56% sidebar statistic.

sidebar.body: of CROs expect AI to enhance productivity within a stable workforce.

items.text: Compared with previous years, CROs expect to hire fewer risk management professionals or reduce their risk management staff.
items.sub: Nearly half (49%) of survey respondents say they will increase the number of FTEs on their teams, down from 68% last year, while 30% say they will decrease their FTEs, up from 16% last year.

items.text: North American CROs are most likely (31%) to expect a modest decrease in staffing (1% - 15% reductions), while CROs at banks in Latin America are least likely (14%).
items.sub: This is the first time such a clear slowdown in hiring trends has been observed.

items.text: AI is one reason for smaller risk management teams, though not the only explanation.
items.sub: The picture is nuanced when it comes to AI’s impact on risk management staffing and roles.

items.text: 16% of survey respondents said they expect significant headcount reduction.
items.sub: For example, due to AI automating repetitive tasks such as data collection and reporting.

items.text: 28% said AI’s impact on the size and composition of the risk workforce was still being evaluated.
56% of CROs expect AI to enhance productivity within a stable workforce.
items.sub: CROs see that it will soon be possible to do more with less, or at least with the same number of staff.

items.text: "AI and digital activity allow us to keep headcount stable despite the increase of activity from the clients."

right.title: Figure 23: Expected change in the number of FTEs in risk management in next three years
